title: MCP server exposes 8 tools including a cross-file player/match join
evidence: src/server.ts registers search_matches, team_record, compare_teams, search_players, competition_standings, dataset_statistics, player_club_context, list_team_competitions; player_club_context (src/tools.ts:180) joins FIFA player data to match records
suggestion: None — beyond-spec coverage, noted for cross-run comparison

title: canonicalMatches() re-run on every tool call over ~24k matches
evidence: src/tools.ts:35,79,92,133,152,184,205 each call canonicalMatches(data.matches) per invocation instead of computing the deduped set once at load
suggestion: Memoize the canonicalized match array in AppData at load time; still well within the <2s/<5s perf budget

title: player_club_context club join is exact-substring on FIFA Club vs match team names
evidence: src/tools.ts:184 teamRecord(canonicalMatches(...), player.club) — FIFA clubs like 'Paris Saint-Germain'/'Liverpool' won't match Brazilian match-data team names, so most non-Brazilian-club players return 'No match data found for <club>'
suggestion: Normalize/alias club names across the two sources, or document that the join only resolves Brazilian clubs present in the match data

title: search_matches requires a mandatory team argument
evidence: src/server.ts search_matches inputSchema marks team as required (z.string()); competition/season-only queries like 'find all Copa do Brasil finals' cannot be answered without naming a team
suggestion: Make team optional so competition/season/date filters can stand alone; R5's competition filter is otherwise satisfied

title: code_quality score is moderate (0.7333)
evidence: scores.json code_quality=0.7333333333333334 (stored by retort scorer); no build/lint re-run performed per skill guidance
suggestion: Informational — build and all 96 tests pass (test_coverage=1.0, defect_rate=1.0); no specific lint failure identified
